input.prompt:
Create a Blazor Web App for building inspectors on construction sites.

Requirements:
- Inspectors fill out safety checklists and add notes at each site visit
- Photos are attached to inspection reports
- Construction sites frequently have poor or no cellular connectivity — the app must continue working offline
- When connectivity returns, data syncs back to the server
- Inspectors use phones and tablets, not desktops
- Low-latency UI is critical — inspectors tap through checklist items quickly
- No authentication needed in the initial version

data.content:
The Blazor WebAssembly PWA is ready. Here's what was built:

**Architecture:**
- **Blazor WebAssembly PWA** — runs entirely client-side, works offline via service worker
- **IndexedDB** (via JS interop) — all data persisted locally on device
- **Background sync** — polls every 30s when online, auto-triggers on reconnect

**Key features:**
| Feature | Implementation |
|---------|---------------|
| Safety checklists | 28-item template across 7 categories (Fall Protection, Scaffolding, Electrical, etc.) |
| Notes | Per-item notes (auto-shown on failures) + general notes tab |
| Photo attachments | Camera capture via `<input capture>`, stored as base64 in IndexedDB |
| Offline-first | Service worker caches all assets; IndexedDB stores all data locally |
| Sync on reconnect | `SyncService` detects connectivity changes, syncs pending inspections |
| Mobile-optimized | Touch targets ≥44px, no-zoom viewport, `100dvh`, safe-area insets |
| Low-latency UI | Immediate local writes, no server round-trips for interactions |

**To run:** `dotnet run` — then open on a phone/tablet and install as a PWA.
